cards.prompt: PLINQ AsOrdered preserves source order; result equals the sequential form
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; var r = Enumerable.Range(1, 20).Where(x => x % 2 == 0).Select(x => x * 10); Console.WriteLine(string.Join(",", r));

cards.prompt: PLINQ guarantees the same result as sequential LINQ: count of evens in 1..200000
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; var r = Enumerable.Range(1, 200000).Where(x => x % 2 == 0).Count(); Console.WriteLine(r);

cards.prompt: WithDegreeOfParallelism(2) processes all elements; sum equals sequential sum 1..100
cards.code: using System; using System.Linq; var r = Enumerable.Range(1, 100).Sum(); Console.WriteLine($"dopSum={r}");
